title: 7 Viewing, Downloading, and Searching for Trace Requests
body_text:
7	Viewing, Downloading, and Searching for Trace Requests
7.1	Overview of Viewing, Downloading, and Searching for Trace Requests
The status of a trace request determines if it is displayed in the OLT Inbox or in the OLT Outbox.
The following types of trace requests are displayed in the OLT Inbox:
Unauthorized trace requests
Biller-initiated trace requests (these initially appear in the Outbox but are closed and moved to the Inbox at the end of the business day)
Closed financial institution-initiated trace requests
Cancelled trace requests
The following types of trace requests are displayed in the OLT Outbox:
Financial institution-initiated trace requests that are in progress (those created by Tracers must be authorized before they appear in the Outbox)
Biller-initiated trace requests (these initially appear in the Outbox but are closed and moved to the Inbox at the end of the business day)
The ten most recent trace requests in the Inbox and Outbox are displayed on the home page. Use one of the OLT search functions to determine if a transaction has been traced, to check the status of a trace request, or to look for updates to the trace request.
7.2	OLT Trace Request Status Types
The status of each trace request is updated as it is processed on OLT. Status types are colour-coded. See the following table for a description of each OLT status type.
Status Type
Description
Location
Waiting for Approval
Indicates that the trace request requires authorization.
Applies to trace requests created by Tracers only.
Inbox
New ГўВҖВ“ Approved
Indicates that the trace request was submitted to Central 1. This status appears once a SuperTracer approves a trace request created by a Tracer, or when a SuperTracer creates a trace request.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces only.
Outbox
Open
a) Indicates that Central 1 is investigating a financial institution-initiated trace request.
b) Indicates that Central 1 entered a biller-initiated trace request on OLT. The trace will be closed and moved to the Inbox on the same day.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces and biller-initiated traces.
Outbox
Open
ГўВҖВ“
Pending Adjustment
Indicates that the trace request will be closed and settled when the next end-of-day process is completed. The status will change to ГўВҖВңClosed
ГўВҖВ“
Pending AdjustmentГўВҖВқ when the first of two end-of-day processes is completed.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces and biller-initiated traces.
Outbox
Closed
ГўВҖВ“
No Settlement
Not a valid status type.
_
Closed ГўВҖВ“ Pending Adjustment
Indicates that the trace request is closed, and if settlement for an adjustment or service charge is required, it will be posted that day. The status changes to ГўВҖВңClosed ГўВҖВ“ SettledГўВҖВқ when the second end-of-day process is completed.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces and biller-initiated traces.
Inbox
Closed ГўВҖВ“ Settled
Indicates that the trace request is closed, and if settlement for an adjustment or service charge is required, it will be posted that day.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces and biller-initiated traces.
Inbox
Closed ГўВҖВ“ Cancelled
Indicates that the trace request was cancelled.
Applies to financial institution-initiated traces and biller-initiated traces.
Inbox
7.3	Viewing, Printing, and Downloading Trace Requests
Tracers and SuperTracers proceed as follows to view, print, and/or download a trace request:
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Option 1 ГўВҖВ“ View Trace Request Details from the Home Page
ГўВҲВ’
If the trace request is displayed on the home page, select the date, vendor (biller) name, membership number, or trace number to view the details of the trace. See Figure 1.
Note:	The ten most recent trace requests in the Inbox and Outbox are displayed on the home page.
Figure 1: Select Trace on Home Page
Option 2 ГўВҖВ“ View Trace Request Details from the List of All Traces in the Inbox or Outbox
ГўВҲВ’
If the trace request isnГўВҖВҷt displayed on the home page, select
Inbox
,
Outbox
,
or
click to view full list
. A list of all trace requests in either the Inbox or the Outbox is displayed. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: View Additional Traces
ГўВҲВ’
Select the arrows in the date, vendor name, membership number, or trace number column to sort the trace requests. See Figure 3.
Note:	The sort option isnГўВҖВҷt available on the home page.
Figure 3: Sort Trace Request
ГўВҲВ’
Select the date, vendor (biller) name, membership number, or trace number to view the details of a trace request. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Select Trace
Option 3 ГўВҖВ“ Use the Search Function to View Trace Request Details
ГўВҲВ’
Select
Search Trace Request
. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Search Function
ГўВҲВ’
Enter the search criteria and select
Quick Search
or
Detail Search
. See Figure 6. The Quick Search function is also available on the main page.
Note:	See Section 7.4, Using the ГўВҖВңSearch Trace RequestГўВҖВқ Function, for more information on search functionality.
Figure 6: Search Criteria
ГўВҲВ’
Select the trace from the search results page to view the trace request details. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Search Result
View the details of the trace request using one of the previously described options to open the trace request details. See Figure 8.
Note:	SuperTracers have access to options that arenГўВҖВҷt available to Tracers.
Figure 8: Trace Request Details
Select
Download as Text
to print a text only version of the trace request or to save it to your computer.
Note:	The Additional Comments field on the trace request isnГўВҖВҷt displayed if it is downloaded as text. If the content of the Additional Comments field is required, copy and paste it into the text document.
Select
Open
or
Save
when prompted. See Figure 9 to view a sample text-only trace request.
Figure 9: Text Only Sample
Use the browserГўВҖВҷs print function to print the details of the trace request.
7.4	Using the ГўВҖВңSearch Trace RequestГўВҖВқ Function
Use the ГўВҖВңSearch Trace RequestГўВҖВқ function to locate trace requests. This function doesnГўВҖВҷt allow the user to create a new trace request.
A Tracer or SuperTracer proceeds as follows to locate a trace using the ГўВҖВңSearch Trace RequestГўВҖВқ function:
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select
Search Trace Request
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Search Trace Request
Enter the trace number of the original transaction and select
Quick Search
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Quick Search
Alternatively, enter detailed search criteria as follows. See Figure 12.
ГўВҲВ’
Start Date (mandatory)
ГўВҖВ“ Select the calendar icon to select a start date.
ГўВҲВ’
End Date (mandatory)
ГўВҖВ“ Select the calendar icon to select an end date.
Note:	As the date range increases, the search response time decreases.
ГўВҲВ’
Trace #
ГўВҖВ“
Enter the trace number of the original transaction.
ГўВҲВ’
Transaction Amount ($)
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the amount of the transaction.
ГўВҲВ’
Vendor Number
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the Central 1 bill payment system number of the biller.
ГўВҲВ’
Member Vendor Account #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs account number with the biller.
ГўВҲВ’
Reference #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the OLT trace reference number.
Figure 12: Enter Search Criteria
Select
Detail Search
.
A list of traces that match the search criteria is displayed. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: Search Results
Select a trace request to view the details.
Note:	Once the details of the trace are displayed, additional options may be available (e.g., ГўВҖВңDownload as TextГўВҖВқ, ГўВҖВңArchive This Trace RequestГўВҖВқ).
7.5	Using the ГўВҖВңSearch TransactionГўВҖВқ Function
A Tracer or SuperTracer proceeds as follows to locate a transaction or existing trace request using the ГўВҖВңSearch TransactionГўВҖВқ function:
Note:	This function is also used to create trace requests. See Section 8.2, Creating Trace Requests on OLT, for procedures.
Note:	Transaction data is available on OLT for one year.
Log in to OLT, as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to OLT.
Select
Search Transaction
. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Search Transaction
Enter search criteria as described below. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Enter Search Criteria
Note:	The start date and amount are required information. At least one of the following fields must also be completed: trace number, vendor name, member account number, or member vendor account number.
ГўВҲВ’
Start Date
ГўВҖВ“ Select the calendar icon to select the transaction date.
ГўВҲВ’
End Date
ГўВҖВ“ If the exact date of the transaction is unknown, select the calendar icon to select an end date. The date range must not exceed seven days.
Note:	As the date range increases, the search response time decreases.
ГўВҲВ’
Amount ($)
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the transaction amount.
ГўВҲВ’
Enter at least one of the following:
ГўВҲВ’
Trace #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the trace number of the original transaction.
ГўВҲВ’
Vendor Name
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the name of the biller. A list of possible matches appears after three or more characters are typed into this field. Select the appropriate biller from the list of matches.
ГўВҲВ’
Member Account #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs financial institution account number.
ГўВҲВ’
Member Vendor Account #
ГўВҖВ“ Enter the memberГўВҖВҷs account number with the biller.
Select
Search
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Search
Select a transaction from the list of matches.
If the transaction was previously traced or is currently under investigation, select
Open Trace Request
to view the other trace request. See Figure 17.
Note:	Only the most recent trace request associated with the transaction is referenced on this screen. To determine if multiple traces are associated with the transaction, use the Search Trace Request
function as described in Section 7.4, Using the ГўВҖВңSearch Trace RequestГўВҖВқ function.
Figure 17: View Existing Trace
Note:	The ГўВҖВңOpen Trace RequestГўВҖВқ option will only appear if the transaction was previously traced.
Note:	If the transaction hasnГўВҖВҷt been traced, or if a previous trace request for the transaction was closed, the user can initiate a new trace request. See Section 8.2, Creating Trace Requests on OLT, for procedures.
END
